---
title: "LRP56: Mechanism model — receptive vocabulary (R) -> word reading (W)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP56 estimates the adjusted association between receptive vocabulary and word reading
using *all three phase transitions* (t1$\to$t2, t2$\to$t3, t3$\to$t4) with
phase-specific intercepts.

::: {.callout-note title="Linear mechanism (estimand note)"}
The mechanism enters as a **linear slope** $\beta_{\text{mech}}$ on
$\text{logit}_*(R_{\text{post}}, N_R)$, not the 1-D HSGP curve used elsewhere in this
family. At reporting tier the nonparametric $f^R$ curve does not converge here (~300
divergences on an otherwise-healthy posterior, and raising `target_accept` does not
help — it is HSGP geometry, not boundary steps). Rather than drop any DAG-required
adjuster to buy convergence, the mechanism is entered linearly, which converges
cleanly (0 divergences); this follows the #258 review's own suggestion. The estimand
is therefore the **linear** $R \to W$ adjusted association — a single slope, not a
shape. Recovering the curve would need a reparameterised GP and is left as follow-up.
The letter-sound mechanism (LRP58) keeps its HSGP curve; only the vocabulary-predictor
mechanisms show this pathology.
:::

## Adjustment set

The model conditions on this DAG-motivated adjustment set to reduce confounding
of the $R_{\text{post}}$--$W_{\text{post}}$ association:

Observed parents of $R$ (RV) under the revised DAG (#245): $\{A, HS, RW, TR\}$.

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on **linearly by default** (a $\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$
  term whenever age is a declared confounder and the age GP is off, as here).
- $TR$ — taught receptive vocabulary, a parent of $R$ that also causes $W$
  (measure confounder). **New under the revised DAG.**
- $HS$ — hearing status (`hs` / `hs_missing`), a new common cause of vocabulary
  skills. **New (#245).**
- $RW$ — phonological memory (`erbto`), now an upstream cause of $R$. **New (#245).**
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading (temporal precedence; not a collider).

$E$ is a *descendant* of $R$ (path $R \to E$) that also affects $W$.
Conditioning on $E$ would change the estimand by removing the $R \to E \to W$
pathway and pulling the estimated $f^R$ toward the direct-only component. $E$ is
therefore **not** in the adjustment set. $GA$ (general ability) is latent and
cannot be conditioned on; the child random intercept proxies its time-invariant
part, so $f^R$ stays an adjusted association.

### Interpretation caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* (NUCA) for $(R_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$: strong and not testable.
- $R_{\text{post}}$ is measured with error; if measurement error is non-negligible, $f^R$ attenuates toward zero (a latent-$R$ extension is out of scope here).
- Across-phase pooling assumes the structural relationship is stable over time; a phase-specific sensitivity fit is available via the `phase_specific_mechanism` flag.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
